summary_one_line: ATTLIST attribute whose local-name (after QName split) equals the literal string xmlns, so ret->name matches BAD_CAST "xmlns".

hypotheses.what_input_feature: Input must declare an attribute whose post-xmlSplitQName local name equals the 5-byte literal "xmlns" exactly (e.g. <!ATTLIST elem xmlns CDATA ...>), so that the equality CMP at valid.c:2134 (xmlStrEqual(ret->name, BAD_CAST "xmlns")) returns true and the branch's true side is taken.
hypotheses.why_winner_satisfies: value_profile_cmplog winner seeds include declarations whose attribute local-name is literally "xmlns" — e.g. Seed 3 at offset 0xd0 shows '<!ATTLIST b xmlns        CDATA' and Seed 1 contains 'xmlns%xlink' that after xmlSplitQName yields ret->name == "xmlns". The DIVERGENT BRANCHES tally at L2134 shows the winner reaches the true side 30 times vs the loser's 0.
hypotheses.why_loser_doesnt: value_profile loser seeds only carry the namespaced form 'xmlns:xlink' where the local-name after QName split is "xlink" and the prefix is "xmlns"; ret->name never equals "xmlns" so the first clause at line 2134 is always false (L T=0 F=60). vp has CMP_MAP Hamming feedback but no I2SRandReplace stage to bulk-substitute the literal token into the local-name position.
hypotheses.mechanism_attribution: I2SRandReplace in value_profile_cmplog logs the runtime CMP operand BAD_CAST "xmlns" via cmplog instrumentation and substitutes it directly into the byte position that becomes ret->name. value_profile lacks the I2S mutator and must rely on Hamming-distance gradient + havoc, which can reach the prefix-form 'xmlns:...' but does not produce the unprefixed local-name form within the budget.

evidence_trail.claim: The blocker is an equality CMP comparing ret->name with the 5-byte string literal "xmlns"; the true side is globally blocked.
The single decisive pair at this branch is value_profile_cmplog > value_profile with delta technique I2S.
Winner resolves on all 10 trials while loser blocks on 9 of 10 trials at this branch.
evidence_trail.exact_quote:
winner: resolved=10/10  blocked=0  unreached=0
  loser:  resolved=1/10  blocked=9  unreached=0

evidence_trail.claim: At the blocker line 2134 the per-role coverage shows winner takes true 30 times and loser takes true 0 times.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: d=1   L2134  T=30 F=66  T=0 F=60  if ((xmlStrEqual(ret->name, BAD_CAST "xmlns")) ||  <-- BLOCKER

evidence_trail.claim: At line 2136 (the second clause checking ret->prefix == "xmlns") the loser reaches the prefix-form true case 21 times while the winner reaches it 0 times, indicating loser only hits the namespaced prefix form and never the unprefixed local-name form required by the line-2134 first clause.
evidence_trail.exact_quote: d=1   L2136  T=0 F=63  T=21 F=36  (xmlStrEqual(ret->prefix, BAD_CAST "xmlns"))))) {

evidence_trail.claim: value_profile lacks the I2SRandReplace mutator that would let it substitute the literal CMP operand into the input byte position.
value_profile_cmplog uniquely combines CMP_MAP gradient feedback with the I2SRandReplace mutator, which is the only one-technique-delta against value_profile.

mechanism_consistency_check.verification_method: Ran `python3 tools/db_query.py lineage --branch 2983 --role W --fuzzer cmplog --trial 2 --seed 761378055f85e12d` and observed 1 I2S-floor ancestor dash row (mutation_op = -) at depth 3 of the 4-level chain. Under the current LibAFL build dash rows are exclusive to cmplog/vpc, so the floor row corroborates an I2SRandReplace substitution somewhere on the path to this winner-resolving seed. Note: the lineage was queried on the cmplog-trial-2 storing fuzzer (one of the two I2S-capable variants); the same I2S mutator is what defines the value_profile_cmplog>value_profile delta.

falsifiability.would_be_refuted_by: A synthetic harness where the gate compares ret->name against a runtime-derived value (not a literal) — under the same scan, value_profile_cmplog should LOSE its edge over value_profile, since I2SRandReplace cannot substitute non-literal operands. If vpc still beat vp on such a harness, the I2S literal-substitution attribution would be refuted.

weakest_evidence_point: The lineage check found a dash row at depth 3 but the cmplog-storing winner trial is provenance only; I cannot directly confirm the I2S substitution event installed the exact bytes "xmlns" into the local-name position (the dash signal is a per-build lower bound on I2S activity).
